name: danniel-claude-plugins
description: Danniel Libor's collection of Claude Code plugins.

plugins.name: claude-dream-team
plugins.description: A summonable dream team of senior expert agents — a DevOps engineer (Docker, industry-standard CI/CD with mandatory manual-approval gates and semver tags/releases for UAT & prod, GCP infra), a fullstack developer (SOLID/clean code, security-first, cross-platform UI/UX, database design), a QA engineer (unit, Playwright E2E, VAPT, code review), and a technical writer (user guides, how-to/feature docs, FAQs, release notes for clients/stakeholders). Includes focused skills (project-adaptation, gcp-devops, senior-development, qa-testing, git-conventions, documentation) and a /dream-team-review command that routes QA findings to the dev/devops agents and re-verifies. Stack-agnostic; composes with each project's CLAUDE.md, skills, and agents.

plugins.name: claude-sdlc-team
plugins.description: Agentic SDLC team (requirements → design → implementation → testing → review → security → release → docs) with per-feature auto-merge to development, sprint close promoting to staging for UAT, a separate production release to main with semantic versioning + tagging, CI/CD pipeline scan-and-fix, and optional per-project Jira-Scrum Notion sync — per-project hub pages, backlog, sprint planning (Definition of Ready), and a scrum-master skill. All stage promotions are PR/MR-based with non-protected branch cleanup. A Playwright E2E layer records a per-feature review video — with a visible mouse cursor, never screenshots — that gates the merge to development.

plugins.name: obsidian-vault-keeper
plugins.description: An active Obsidian vault steward: the vault-keeper agent maintains your vault to best practices through the Obsidian MCP (CLI or filesystem fallback) — captures and files notes into PARA, builds and refreshes MOC index notes, fixes broken [[links]] and finds orphans, normalizes frontmatter, safely dedupes/renames, and runs the archive lifecycle. Includes three skills — obsidian-vault (the official Obsidian style guide, PARA organization, MCP-aware maintenance workflows, Dataview/Templates), vault-conventions (this vault's own house rules: the folder map, where each incoming note goes, capture-vs-harvest for daily and meeting notes, and the #reusable carry-forward tag), and project-context (the repo ↔ vault bridge: coding sessions read the matching project note's business rules before changing domain logic and write discoveries back) — plus /vault-checkup (read-only health sweep), /vault-harvest (promote #reusable captures into reusable knowledge), and /vault-wire (scaffold and wire a repo's project note) commands. Complements the required official obsidian plugin (by Steph Ango/kepano) — it owns vault stewardship and defers Obsidian authoring syntax (Markdown, Bases, Canvas) to that plugin's skills.
